items.title: Paris Saint-Germain hengelt Barcelona-parel Dro binnen
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain heeft maandagavond Dro Fernández voorgesteld als nieuwste aanwinst. Het achttienjarige talent is overgenomen van Barcelona, dat de aanvallende middenvelder liever had behouden. Fernández kiest echter voor iets nieuws.

items.title: Everton v Leeds United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Premier League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Mail Michael We are underway! The teams are out! Everton in their blue shirts and white shirts, blue socks. Leeds in their famous all-white kit. Continue reading...

items.title: Bayern Munich open talks to extend Kane contract
items.summary: Bayern Munich sporting director Max Eberl says the German club are in talks with Harry Kane about extending the striker's contract.

items.title: ADO wint wéér niet, weergaloze treffer Rijkhoff bij klimmend Almere City
items.summary: ADO Den Haag is het even kwijt. De koploper van de Keuken Kampioen Divisie verloor zijn laatste drie wedstrijden en kwam maandag niet verder dan een doelpuntloos gelijkspel tegen Vitesse. Almere City won een doelpuntenfestijn in Wijdewormer en passeerde daarmee De Graafschap.

items.title: Pastoor ziet weinig toekomst voor Ajax-talent Bouwman: 'Hij kan niet verdedigen'
items.summary: Aaron Bouwman (18) kan niet op lof rekenen van Alex Pastoor. De analyticus had weinig op met de Ajacied. Volgens de clubloze trainer kan het talent niet verdedigen.

items.title: Dit kan PSV verwachten van Bayern München
items.summary: Wil PSV zich verzekeren van de tussenronde in de Champions League, dan dient het af te rekenen met de best aanvallende ploeg ter wereld. Want momenteel is er geen club zo explosief als Bayern München. Analyse van de angstloze speelwijze van Vincent Kompany.

items.title: Buijs schiet in de lach na tirade Vaessen: 'Dit is toch genieten?'
items.summary: Danny Buijs heeft maandagavond gereageerd op de uitlatingen van Etienne Vaessen. De doelman van FC Groningen vond het voetbal van de trainer van Fortuna Sittard niet om aan te zien . Dat zorgde voor een lach bij de oefenmeester.

items.title: 'Surprisingly productive' - Hadjar's verdict as Red Bull top first testing day
items.summary: Isack Hadjar admitted his surprise at Red Bull's "productive" and "smooth" performance after topping the timesheet at the first day of Formula 1 pre-season testing in Barcelona.

items.title: Carragher: Slot's job at risk if Liverpool miss out on Champions League
items.summary: Jamie Carragher believes Arne Slot's job will be at risk if Liverpool fail to qualify for the Champions League this season.

items.title: Carrick’s cult of common sense gets Manchester United believing again
items.summary: There is no special trick to the interim manager’s early success, just a commitment to sound and sensible thinking What must Ruben Amorim make of it? Maybe that 3-4-2-1 might not be the answer for this Manchester United team? Perish the thought. The club’s recently sacked manager was clear that not even the pope would make him change – presumably because Leo XIV is also a big fan of three centre-halves. Saying that, Amorim did come close to losing his religion towards the bitter end, however brief and unconvincing his dalliance with a back four was. He reverted to a three for his final game at Leeds in early January. As the dust settles on Michael Carrick’s second thrilling win as the United interim manager in two matches, the last-gasp triumph at Arsenal following the home win against Manchester City, it is a moment, first and foremost, for the club’s supporters to savour. Continue reading...

items.title: Nottingham Forest make £35m bid for Crystal Palace striker Jean-Philippe Mateta
items.summary: French forward has told Palace he wants to leave Forest also interested in Wolves’ Strand Larsen Nottingham Forest have made a £35m bid to sign Jean-Philippe Mateta from Crystal Palace. The France striker, who has told Palace he wants to leave, is among Forest’s targets as they look to strengthen in attack. Last week Forest signed the 6ft 7in forward Lorenzo Lucca on loan from Napoli with an option to make the deal permanent. Only Wolves have scored fewer top-flight goals this season. Continue reading...

items.title: Everton make HT changes with 'excellent' Leeds in front LIVE!
Celtic to appeal Trusty red at Hearts
items.summary: Celtic will appeal the red card shown to Auston Trusty during Sunday's 2-2 draw at Hearts.

items.title: Sources: Brazil asks FIFA to host Club World Cup
items.summary: Brazil has asked FIFA to host the 2029 FIFA Club World Cup, sources told ESPN Brazil, saying that the request came during a meeting of top Brazil football officials during a visit to Rio de Janeiro by FIFA president Gianni Infantino on Monday.

items.title: Endrick after hat trick: This is just the beginning
items.summary: Endrick feels vindicated to have moved to Lyon after scoring the first hat-trick of his career in the club's 5-2 league win at Metz.

items.title: Report: Harry Kane in talks for new Bayern deal
items.summary: Harry Kane has opened talks over a new Bayern Munich contract, according to a report in Germany.

items.title: Gotham signs FSU, U.S. U20 star Jordynn Dudley
items.summary: American forward Jordynn Dudley, a two-time NCAA champion with Florida State University, has signed for Gotham FC.

items.title: One week until transfer deadline day - who could be on the move?
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at some of the players that have been linked with moves before the Premier League transfer deadline on 2 February at 19:00 GMT.

items.title: Forest prepared to pay £35m for Palace's Mateta
items.summary: Nottingham Forest are prepared to pay £35m to sign Jean-Philippe Mateta from Crystal Palace and are willing to offer the striker a significant pay rise.

items.title: Mature decision to deny Manchester City a penalty for handball sets example for referees | Chris Foy
items.summary: Farai Hallam trusted his own judgment on Yerson Mosquera incident despite a VAR review and, in doing so, showed the way forward I refereed professional football for 25 years. We were talking about handball when I started and it remains one of the most discussed topics in the game. One reason for this is that we’ve had a number of law changes by the International Football Association Board (Ifab) over recent years. All were made in an effort to achieve consistent outcomes for the benefit of the game, but we can sometimes end up with different interpretations of the laws. Continue reading...
